outputSteps.content:
The multi-tenant development environment has been created and secured as requested. Summary of what’s deployed:

Namespaces
- Created: dev-alice, dev-bob, dev-charlie, dev-shared, staging, prod
- Labeled for policy scoping: dev namespaces have env=dev and scope=dev; dev-shared has env=dev and scope=shared; staging has env=staging and scope=restricted; prod has env=prod and scope=restricted

RBAC
- Per-developer ServiceAccounts: alice-sa (dev-alice), bob-sa (dev-bob), charlie-sa (dev-charlie)
- Full access to own namespace:
  - RoleBindings in dev-alice/dev-bob/dev-charlie bind:
    - User identities: alice, bob, charlie
    - Their respective ServiceAccounts
    - To ClusterRole admin (full namespace admin)
- Read-only to dev-shared:
  - RoleBinding in dev-shared binds:
    - Users alice, bob, charlie
    - ServiceAccounts alice-sa, bob-sa, charlie-sa
    - To ClusterRole view (read-only)
- Staging/prod: No developer access was granted. By default, only cluster-admins (system:masters) have access.

ResourceQuotas
- dev-alice/dev-bob/dev-charlie:
  - limits.cpu=2, limits.memory=4Gi, requests.cpu=2, requests.memory=4Gi, pods=10, services=5
- dev-shared:
  - limits.cpu=4, limits.memory=8Gi, requests.cpu=4, requests.memory=8Gi, pods=20, services=10
- staging/prod:
  - limits.cpu=8, limits.memory=16Gi, requests.cpu=8, requests.memory=16Gi, pods=50, services=20

NetworkPolicies (requires a CNI that enforces NetworkPolicy, e.g., Calico/Cilium/Antrea/Weave)
- Default deny (ingress and egress) for all created namespaces: dev-alice, dev-bob, dev-charlie, dev-shared, staging, prod
- Allow same-namespace traffic in each namespace (ingress and egress within the namespace)
- DNS and system services:
  - Egress on TCP/UDP 53 to kube-system (CoreDNS)
  - Broad egress to kube-system allowed (system services)
- Dev namespaces can reach dev-shared:
  - In dev-* namespaces: egress allowed to namespace dev-shared
  - In dev-shared: ingress allowed from namespaces labeled env=dev, scope=dev
- Cross-developer isolation:
  - No egress from one dev namespace to another (other than dev-shared)
- Staging/prod isolation:
  - Default deny both ways; only same-namespace traffic and egress to DNS/system services are allowed

Notes and recommendations
- Users alice, bob, charlie must exist in your cluster’s auth provider with the same usernames for RBAC to apply.
- Kubernetes v1.24+: ServiceAccount tokens are not auto-created. To get a token for a developer’s SA:
  - kubectl -n dev-alice create token alice-sa
  - kubectl -n dev-bob create token bob-sa
  - kubectl -n dev-charlie create token charlie-sa
- To verify RBAC quickly:
  - kubectl auth can-i --as alice --namespace dev-alice '*' '*'           -> yes
  - kubectl auth can-i --as alice --namespace dev-bob get pods           -> no
  - kubectl auth can-i --as alice --namespace dev-shared get pods        -> yes
  - kubectl auth can-i --as alice --namespace staging get pods           -> no
- To verify network isolation:
  - Run a test pod in dev-alice and try to curl a service/pod in dev-bob (should fail)
  - Curl a service in dev-shared from dev-alice/dev-bob/dev-charlie (should succeed)
  - From staging/prod, test that traffic to dev* namespaces is blocked; DNS queries should succeed

If you want tighter “system services” egress, we can replace the broad kube-system egress with precise policies that only allow egress to CoreDNS and any specific system endpoints you require.

results.taskOutput:
Done.

- Created namespace statefulset-test, a headless Service db, and a 3-replica StatefulSet db. Each pod mounts a 1Gi PVC at /data and an init container writes the file /data/test with the string initial_data if it doesn’t already exist.
- Scaled the StatefulSet back down to 1 replica.

Notes and verification:
- The PVCs for db-1 and db-2 are retained on scale-down, preserving their data.
- To verify, you can exec into db-0 and check:
  - kubectl exec -n statefulset-test db-0 -- sh -c 'cat /data/test'
- If you want me to wait for all pods to become Ready and verify the file contents across replicas before/after scaling, say the word.

outputSteps.content:
Done.

- Created namespace statefulset-test, a headless Service db, and a 3-replica StatefulSet db. Each pod mounts a 1Gi PVC at /data and an init container writes the file /data/test with the string initial_data if it doesn’t already exist.
- Scaled the StatefulSet back down to 1 replica.

Notes and verification:
- The PVCs for db-1 and db-2 are retained on scale-down, preserving their data.
- To verify, you can exec into db-0 and check:
  - kubectl exec -n statefulset-test db-0 -- sh -c 'cat /data/test'
- If you want me to wait for all pods to become Ready and verify the file contents across replicas before/after scaling, say the word.
